title: platyspermum alatum
parts: Platyspermum alatum has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and stems. The leaves of Platyspermum alatum contain flavonoids like quercetin, and the roots contain saponins and triterpenoids. The stems of this plant contain alkaloids and phenolic compounds. Unfortunately, specific information on the medicinal constituents of the flowers of Platyspermum alatum is not readily available.
side_effects: Platyspermum alatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems, particularly when ingested or inhaled. In some cases, excessive consumption of the plant may lead to stomach pain, nausea, and vomiting. Additionally, improper use can cause liver and kidney damage due to the presence of toxic compounds. It is essential to consult a medical professional or an experienced herbalist before using Platyspermum alatum for medicinal purposes.
